features.summary: Johnson & Johnson reported Q3 2021 worldwide sales of $23.3 billion, up 10.7% year-over-year, with adjusted diluted EPS of $2.60 representing 18.2% growth. Pharmaceuticals grew 13.2% driven by strong performance from STELARA, TREMFYA, and DARZALEX, while Medical Devices grew 7% despite COVID-19 Delta variant impacts on elective procedures. Consumer Health increased 4.1% with particular strength in over-the-counter medicines. The company tightened full-year 2021 adjusted operational sales guidance to 12.9%-13.5% and raised adjusted EPS guidance to $9.65-$9.70.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued recovery in medical device markets despite ongoing hospital staffing shortages and COVID-19 variability. They anticipate strong pharmaceutical growth trajectory to continue into 2022, particularly for key brands like DARZALEX and ERLEADA. The company remains committed to R&D investment, increasing spending 23% year-to-date, and expects to launch their BCMA CAR-T therapy in Q4 2021. Management views current COVID-related headwinds as temporary and maintains confidence in long-term market attractiveness across all segments.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing COVID-19 impacts on elective medical procedures, particularly in spine and knee surgeries, and hospital staffing shortages constraining procedure volumes globally. The company faces significant litigation exposure with a $2.1 billion charge for talc-related claims and $800 million for RISPERDAL settlements. Technical development challenges led to a $900 million impairment on the Ottava robotic surgery platform, with first-in-human trials delayed approximately two years. Supply chain constraints and competitive pressures in certain pharmaceutical markets also present ongoing challenges.
features.key_drivers: Growth is driven by strong pharmaceutical performance across immunology (STELARA up 21.7%, TREMFYA up 63.5%) and oncology (DARZALEX up 42.9%, ERLEADA up 65.8%) franchises. Medical devices benefited from procedure recovery and new product launches, with 17 significant product introductions year-to-date. Consumer health growth was supported by increased demand for trusted OTC brands and category recovery. The COVID-19 vaccine contributed $502 million in Q3 revenue, bringing year-to-date total to $766 million at not-for-profit pricing.
